text:
At the heart of a quaint English village lived a fox named Freddy. Freddy was not your typical fox—it loved reading classics and often wore a monocle, which he seemed to polish before each nap. One grey morning, after finishing a particularly thrilling adaptation of 'Jane Eyre', Freddy took to the rooftops.

The sky was as clear as crystal. He marveled at how quiet it was, unheard of even for this solitary nocturnal dweller. Above the village, the leaves were still—it had been a sleepless night. And then, in a rare stretch of calm curiosity, Freddy glanced down at his reflection in a dew-kissed pane.

Freddy read aloud to himself, his voice low and melodic. "Oh dear, to be captured, eh? Perhaps there's a story there."

With a hop and a gambol, he sprang from the rooftop. Down he went with precision, swiftly avoiding the watchful eyes of nocturnal birds. He scampered through
